statusLine: Americas oil markets face elevated prices with WTI at $104.54/bbl amid Middle East supply disruptions, while US crude inventories decline and production holds steady at 13.6 million bpd.
fullAnalysis:
US petroleum markets are showing strain with WTI crude trading at $104.54/bbl, down modestly by 1.07% but still elevated due to ongoing Middle East supply disruptions. The unusual $14.16 premium of WTI over Brent crude reflects significant supply route disruptions affecting international flows, particularly through the Red Sea and Strait of Hormuz chokepoints. US commercial crude stocks fell by 4.1 million barrels to 409.2 million barrels, indicating tighter domestic supply conditions despite steady production at 13.6 million bpd.

Critical maritime advisories highlight severe risks to global oil flows, with Houthi attacks in the Red Sea/Bab el Mandeb and Iranian threats in the Strait of Hormuz forcing costly rerouting of tanker traffic. These disruptions are keeping international crude prices elevated and creating the inverted WTI-Brent spread, as Americas crude becomes relatively more valuable when Middle Eastern and Russian supplies face transport constraints.

US retail fuel prices remain elevated with gasoline at $4.25/gallon and diesel at $5.61/gallon, reflecting the upstream crude cost pressures. Product inventories are declining across categories, with gasoline stocks falling 6.3 million barrels and distillate stocks down 3.1 million barrels, suggesting robust demand is outpacing refinery output. The Strategic Petroleum Reserve remains at historically low levels of 78 million barrels, limiting the administration's ability to buffer market volatility.

Across the broader Americas region, these elevated prices benefit producers like Canada's oil sands operators and Brazil's Petrobras, though pipeline constraints continue to limit Canadian export capacity. Guyana's rapidly expanding production provides some supply cushion, while Venezuela remains largely sidelined by sanctions despite holding massive reserves.
keyPoints: WTI crude at $104.54/bbl trades at unusual $14.16 premium over Brent due to Middle East supply disruptions
US crude inventories fell 4.1 million barrels while production holds steady at 13.6 million bpd
Critical maritime threats in Red Sea and Strait of Hormuz are forcing costly tanker rerouting
US retail gasoline at $4.25/gallon reflects upstream crude cost pressures
Product inventories declining with gasoline stocks down 6.3 million barrels
Strategic Petroleum Reserve at low 78 million barrels limits policy response options
